text:
Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Lily who lived in a peculiar town where the only way to watch fireworks was to go abroad on vacation every year. Every summer, Lily dreamt of fireworks soaring across the sky. One day, after too many nights staring up aimlessly at the dark sky, she decided to build her own model fireworks display.

Lily spent nights assembling intricate papier-mâché fireworks rockets, setting them aflame with tiny flares of candle wax. Each morning she would practice launching them into the clouds, imagining them bursting into colorful fireworks. She turned her kitchen into a makeshift fireworks hangar, organizing them by color and design.

It wasn't long before her little garden grew mysterious fireworks that sparkled softly under the moonlight. People passing by would stop to watch, fascinated by the tiny lights that twinkled like stars gone to sleep on leafy little patrollers.

Days turned into weeks, and soon Lily had a small but enthusiastic village visiting her
